flags.rewound_from_stage0_5_pivot: Case 6b — Stage 0.5 reject fast-path: the reviewer's grounds are conjecture-level (Conjectures 1 and 2 are refuted by the artifact's own audit since the advertised P-star witness has empty K=2 sparse feasible class — unit 2's four distinct a=0 neighbor-state means 0.33/0.44/0.40/0.51 cannot be represented by two exposure labels, so the claimed 1/40 gaps are not valid). The only positive result that survives is a finite Farkas/enumeration certificate, which the reviewer classifies as below the flagship floor (subfield/incremental). This is kernel_substituted, not a derivation slip — re-deriving on the same .tex would either re-discover the same refutation or yield the same sub-flagship certificate.

proposed_from.topic: Random-set sharp partial identification of direct and indirect treatment effects under sparse-network interference when the exposure mapping support is unknown but bounded in cardinality
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art is pid_network_exposure/v1, whose headline is shared-completion LP bounds for network ADEs with cellwise nonsharpness and capacitated endpoint certificates. The present proposal differs by fixing a total policy contrast t and constructing the joint direct-indirect compatibility set R_K(P;t); it is not another ADE endpoint or certificate problem. The Stage -1.1 map also names pid_mediation_proxy/v1 and pid_ot_dte_marginal/v1 as adjacent random-set/nonrectangularity substrate, but neither contains sparse-network latent exposure labels or a Hu-Li-Wager style total-decomposition constraint.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are HuLiWager2022 for the direct+indirect=policy-effect decomposition, SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 for sharp bounds under fixed canonical exposure misspecification, and BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 for random-set sharp-region machinery. The gap is their intersection: a support-function characterization of the sharp joint direct-indirect decomposition set when exposure labels are latent K-support objects compatible with sparse networks and a fixed total policy contrast."}
seed_list: Construct the K-sparse exposure random-set envelope C_K(P) and conjecture it is the sharp convex support region for the joint direct/indirect vector over all sparse K-valued latent exposure maps.

Prove generic nonrectangularity of joint direct/indirect bounds via the direct-indirect coupling defect Delta_K(P), showing separately sharp components are not jointly attainable under one latent map.
Give finite sparse-network support certificates for rejected direct/indirect support endpoints, reducing K=2 exposure demands to capacitated set-cover or b-matching certificates.
Characterize a dichotomy for exposure-map specification testing: K-bounded sparse classes are either testable under separation or inherit Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang impossibility.
Build an equivalence-class sharp region over candidate networks and K-bounded exposure maps rather than assuming one correct network or map is in hand.
Develop support-function limit theory for estimated random-set bounds under approximate sparse-network interference with nonsmooth maxima over latent K-support maps.
Solve the K_i=1 finite certificate family for infeasible network-exposure endpoints before extending to capacitated hidden-edge systems.
Prove strict open-set nonsharpness of unitwise exposure-propensity relaxations for network ADE bounds due to shared hidden-edge compatibility.
Give sharp joint bounds for decomposing a total policy effect into direct and indirect components when exposure labels are latent finite-support objects.
Show that Schroder-Oprescu-Feuerriegel-Kallus fixed-canonical exposure bounds are strict outer envelopes of the unknown K-support sparse-map random-set region when label compatibility binds.

seed_details.one_line: Construct the K-sparse exposure random-set envelope C_K(P) and conjecture it is the sharp convex support region for the joint direct/indirect vector over all sparse K-valued latent exposure maps.

anchor_paper.author: Beresteanu, Molchanov, Molinari; Schroder, Oprescu, Feuerriegel, Kallus
anchor_paper.claim: BMM characterize sharp regions by Aumann expectations; Schroder et al. give sharp fixed-canonical exposure misspecification bounds.
anchor_paper.location: BMM pp. 1785-1821; Schroder et al. abstract/partial-ID framework

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP warning avoided: the focal object is a non-LP random-set support envelope with finite LP evaluation only after fixing a map.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 because every incomplete model can be written as a random set.
seed_details.rebuttal: BMM supplies machinery, not the sparse K-support network exposure random set, label normalization, or joint direct/indirect support functional.

adversarial_referee.instance: No named focal object beyond a generic identified set.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by naming C_K(P), h_K(u;P), and Delta_K(P) as focal objects.

adversarial_referee.instance: BMM may already imply the result as a corollary.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch is to state the epsilon-gap as sparse exposure-label specialization plus label-switch normalization.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only one direct effect might be identified, not the whole family.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebutted by stacking all K direct and K(K-1) indirect contrasts jointly.

seed_details.one_line: Prove generic nonrectangularity of joint direct/indirect bounds via the direct-indirect coupling defect Delta_K(P), showing separately sharp components are not jointly attainable under one latent map.

anchor_paper.author: Hu, Li, Wager; Schroder, Oprescu, Feuerriegel, Kallus
anchor_paper.claim: Hu et al. define direct/indirect decomposition; Schroder et al. give separately sharp direct/spillover bounds under fixed misspecification settings.
anchor_paper.location: Hu et al. abstract; Schroder et al. abstract

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/sharpness warning addressed by requiring an open nondegenerate class and named defect Delta_K(P), not one hand-tuned witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just standard nonrectangularity of partial-ID sets because joint extrema rarely combine.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap is tied to one latent sparse exposure map and a computable direct-indirect support defect, not generic convex geometry.

adversarial_referee.instance: The nonrectangularity witness might use collapsed treatment cells.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Draft adds K=2,n=4 witness with probabilities and means bounded away from 0 and 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Delta_K(P) could be tautological.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Defined independently as a support-function difference over a fixed audit direction class.

adversarial_referee.instance: Hu-Li-Wager do not claim componentwise sharpness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: They are decomposition consumers, while Schroder et al. are the componentwise partial-ID comparator.

seed_details.one_line: Give finite sparse-network support certificates for rejected direct/indirect support endpoints, reducing K=2 exposure demands to capacitated set-cover or b-matching certificates.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith proposal pid_network_exposure/v1
anchor_paper.claim: The proposal names b-matching, cover, odd-set, and lifted-cover certificates but leaves exact certificate families open.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends prior certificate idea but nontrivial because endpoints now arise from unknown K-support direct/indirect support directions, not fixed ADE cells.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a pure integer-programming certificate, not a causal identification theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The causal contribution is the reduction from latent direct/indirect support endpoints to graph-realizability certificates.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims random-set motif but delivers only LP certificates.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Certificate is secondary to the Aumann-support envelope and rejects support endpoints.

adversarial_referee.instance: May provide sufficient certificates but not necessity.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Seed is phrased as necessary-and-sufficient endpoint rejection certificates.

adversarial_referee.instance: No computable evaluation map.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: K=2 reduction names capacitated set-cover/b-matching certificate family.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize a dichotomy for exposure-map specification testing: K-bounded sparse classes are either testable under separation or inherit Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang impossibility.

anchor_paper.author: Gao, Harshaw, Savje, Wang; Savje; Weinstein, Nevo
anchor_paper.claim: Gao et al. prove broad exposure-map testing impossibility; Savje separates estimand definitions from structural exposure assumptions; Weinstein-Nevo show network structures need not be unique.
anchor_paper.location: Gao et al. abstract; Savje2024 abstract; WeinsteinNevo2026 abstract

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern: stronger if K-bounded sparsity creates an iff frontier, weaker if only another sufficient test.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already ruled out by GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 because their impossibility covers larger alternatives.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed asks whether sparse K-support restrictions plus separation escape or exactly extend that impossibility.

adversarial_referee.instance: May prove only impossibility or only sufficiency.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The dichotomy must state both sides or be downgraded.

adversarial_referee.instance: Gao et al. may already discuss bounded supports.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Requires close read before pivot; not selected as primary.

adversarial_referee.instance: No causal estimand bounds if only testing.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need tie to identified-set distinguishability; otherwise field-tier.

seed_details.one_line: Build an equivalence-class sharp region over candidate networks and K-bounded exposure maps rather than assuming one correct network or map is in hand.

anchor_paper.author: Weinstein, Nevo; Yu, Airoldi, Borgs, Chayes
anchor_paper.claim: Weinstein-Nevo robustify over candidate networks; Yu et al. study total treatment effects with unknown networks.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: New-object shape: equivalence classes are nontrivial only if they yield a sharp envelope, not just a union of candidate networks.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just taking a union over candidate networks from WeinsteinNevo2026.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed object is a sharp direct/indirect support region over network-map equivalence classes, not robust unbiased estimation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Equivalence class may not be a computable object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs support-function or algorithmic representative enumeration.

adversarial_referee.instance: Weinstein-Nevo target unbiased estimators, not sharp sets.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: That difference is the point; cite as network-misspecification comparator only.

adversarial_referee.instance: May identify only total effect.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Seed must jointly stack direct and indirect effects.

seed_details.one_line: Develop support-function limit theory for estimated random-set bounds under approximate sparse-network interference with nonsmooth maxima over latent K-support maps.

anchor_paper.author: Leung; Savje, Aronow, Hudgens; Li, Wager
anchor_paper.claim: These papers study approximate or limited unknown interference and random graph asymptotics for point estimators or generalized estimands.
anchor_paper.location: Leung2022 abstract; SavjeAronowHudgens2021 abstract; LiWager2022 abstract from Stage -1.1

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends point-estimator asymptotics to random-set endpoints; nontrivial only if nonsmooth support-function maximum is handled.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is inference rather than identification, and CausalSmith is asking for a proposal kernel in PartialID.
seed_details.rebuttal: Could be framed as support-function identified-set inference, but not selected because primary topic is identification.

seed_details.one_line: Solve the K_i=1 finite certificate family for infeasible network-exposure endpoints before extending to capacitated hidden-edge systems.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith proposal pid_network_exposure/v1
anchor_paper.claim: Reviewer explicitly suggests starting with K_i=1 matching/cover certificates before claiming the fully capacitated b-matching case.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens scope from capacitated to K_i=1 but nontrivial because it seeks exact finite necessary-and-sufficient certificates.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is too close to the banked pid_network_exposure open problem.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is useful as a fallback but less novel than the random-set K-support envelope.

seed_details.one_line: Prove strict open-set nonsharpness of unitwise exposure-propensity relaxations for network ADE bounds due to shared hidden-edge compatibility.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith proposal pid_network_exposure/v1
anchor_paper.claim: The prior proposal states Theta_I(P) subsetneq Theta_cell(P) as open but leaves strict containment and minimal witness size unresolved.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/impossibility pattern is strong if promoted to open-set finite-experiment witness; weak if one degenerate law.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is exactly the prior proposal's unproved conjecture.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is a viable pivot only if expanded to unknown K-support maps or a new minimal witness theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could rely on a single four-unit corner law.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Requires open-set witness with nondegenerate observable cells.

adversarial_referee.instance: Prior proposal already owns the kernel.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Not selected as primary to avoid repo-axis duplication.

adversarial_referee.instance: No new object beyond strict containment.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need a named minimal witness or compatibility defect.

seed_details.one_line: Give sharp joint bounds for decomposing a total policy effect into direct and indirect components when exposure labels are latent finite-support objects.

anchor_paper.author: Hu, Li, Wager; Hudgens, Halloran; Hoshino, Yanagi
anchor_paper.claim: Hu et al. decompose a policy effect into direct and indirect components under specified exposure structure; Hoshino-Yanagi consider unknown interference and exposure misspecification.
anchor_paper.location: HuLiWager2022 abstract; HudgensHalloran2008 definitions; HoshinoYanagi2024 abstract

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Joint-family pattern: strong because it targets the joint direct/indirect set, weak if it reports separate intervals only.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a rephrasing of the chosen seed without the random-set support object.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; it is a lower-scored variant unless paired with C_K(P) and Delta_K(P).

adversarial_referee.instance: Direct and indirect components may not identify total effect jointly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State the whole vector and compatibility set, not separate pieces.

adversarial_referee.instance: No evaluation map if only qualitative bounds.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs support-function representation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Hu-Li-Wager define policy decomposition, not partial-ID bounds.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use them as consumer, not as direct prior-art theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Show that Schroder-Oprescu-Feuerriegel-Kallus fixed-canonical exposure bounds are strict outer envelopes of the unknown K-support sparse-map random-set region when label compatibility binds.

anchor_paper.author: Schroder, Oprescu, Feuerriegel, Kallus; Manski
anchor_paper.claim: Schroder et al. give fixed misspecified-exposure sharp bounds; Manski studies weak-restriction social-interaction partial ID.
anchor_paper.location: Schroder et al. abstract; Manski2013 social interactions setup

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Comparator-chain shape: strong if it proves a strict frontier between fixed-canonical, componentwise, and K-support random-set bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: Unknown support should widen, not tighten, fixed-canonical bounds, so the containment direction may be wrong.
seed_details.rebuttal: The claim concerns joint sparse-map compatibility versus product/canonical outer envelopes; direction must be checked in Stage 0.

adversarial_referee.instance: May compare bounds with different targets.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must align label-normalized direct/spillover target before claiming containment.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could prove only one inclusion.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs strict frontier and witness direction.

adversarial_referee.instance: Schroder et al. might already optimize over misspecification classes.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Not primary; requires close paper read.

proposed_from.literature_map: Using Stage -1.1 gaps payload: HudgensHalloran2008 defines direct/indirect effects under partial interference; AronowSamii2017 gives known exposure-map design framework; Manski2013 studies partial ID with social interactions; BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 and BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012 give Aumann/random-set sharp-region machinery; SavjeAronowHudgens2021 and Leung2022 study unknown/approximate interference; HuLiWager2022 links direct and indirect effects to infinitesimal policy effects; YuAiroldiBorgsChayes2022 studies total effects with unknown network; HoshinoYanagi2024 handles unknown interference and exposure-map misspecification; Savje2024 separates exposure definitions from structural assumptions; WeinsteinNevo2026 studies network misspecification and robust estimation; SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 is the closest partial-ID comparator with sharp bounds under misspecified but fixed canonical exposures; GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 proves broad exposure-map testing impossibility. Tensions: fixed exposure maps versus latent finite-support maps; componentwise direct/spillover bounds versus joint direct/indirect regions; random-set incomplete-model machinery versus sparse network exposure compatibility.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff:
{"status":"completed","mode":"pivot","version":1,"message":"Proposes the policy-decomposition compatibility set R_K(P;t) and conjectures it is the sharp direct-indirect identified set under latent K-support sparse-network exposure maps.","literature_map":"Hudgens & Halloran (2008, JASA) give foundational direct, indirect, total, and overall effects under partial interference.
Manski (1993, ReStud) frames the reflection/reference-group problem; Manski (2013, Econometrics Journal) brings partial identification to treatment response with social interactions.
Bramoulle, Djebbari & Fortin (2009, Journal of Econometrics) provide peer-effect identification conditions in linear network models.
Aronow & Samii (2017, AOAS) establish the design/exposure-map/estimand framework under known exposure mappings.
Basse & Airoldi (2018, Sociological Methodology) show design-based limits under arbitrary or weakly structured interference.
Forastiere, Airoldi & Mealli (2021, JASA) and Forastiere et al. (2022, JMLR) develop observational-network direct/spillover estimation under neighborhood interference and generalized propensity scores.
Savje, Aronow & Hudgens (2021, Annals of Statistics) study average effects under unknown but limited interference.
Leung (2022, Econometrica) introduces approximate neighborhood interference for sparse growing networks.
Hu, Li & Wager (2022, Biometrika) define average direct and indirect effects whose sum matches an infinitesimal policy effect.
Yu, Airoldi, Borgs & Chayes (2022, PNAS) handle total treatment effects when the network is unknown, but not direct/indirect sharp bounds.
Hoshino & Yanagi (2024, JASA) consider unknown interference and noncompliance with exposure mappings of instruments.
Savje (2024, Biometrika) separates exposure mappings as estimand definitions from assumptions about the true interference structure.
Weinstein & Nevo (2026, Biometrics) separate network misspecification from mapping misspecification and robustify over candidate networks.
Schroder et al. (2026, arXiv) is the closest partial-ID anchor, giving sharp bounds for misspecified exposure mappings in fixed canonical settings.
Gao, Harshaw, Savje & Wang (2026, arXiv) show broad exposure-map specification testing is impossible without additional restrictions.","seeds":["Construct the K-sparse exposure random-set envelope banked_reason: Stage -1.2 NO-PASS @ flagship (D-1.2 effort=high). 22 reviewer rounds across 4 angles; one angle cleared D-0.5 ACCEPT@flagship, then D-0.5→0 review fast-pathed REJECT (Case 6b kernel_substituted): the advertised P-star witness for K=2 sparse exposure had an empty feasible class — unit 2's four distinct a=0 neighbor-state means (0.33/0.44/0.40/0.51) cannot be represented by two exposure labels, so the claimed 1/40 sharp gaps are not valid. Surviving finite Farkas/enumeration certificate is sound but subfield/incremental. High-effort drafter wrote a more concrete witness (good), but the construction was self-refuting (caught by artifact audit, not concealed).
